title: Strolid Leadership Call
summary: The Strolid Leadership Call covered progress on key initiatives like real-time VCON, Sophia AI, and SOC 2 compliance. Thomas Howe offered due diligence expertise for the Call Review process, which is expected to reach a decision within 30 days, while Joe Furnari is preparing the data room and financial outlook for Call Review. The team also discussed sales strategies, emphasizing live demos and transparency.

decisions.description: Joe Furnari will set up the data room and prepare historical financial data and a three-year outlook for Call Review.
Thomas Howe and Sergey Lyapustin will attend the upcoming meeting with Call Review's technical and product team.
The SOC 2 audit target completion date is set for September, slightly delayed due to the decision to run two separate SOC audits.
The team will run two separate SOC audits to separate VCON and the parent company downstream.
Sales team will get deep into understanding the business operations

actionItems.task: Sign a contract with the auditors for the SOC 2 audit.

expectedOutcome.description: A signed contract with the auditors for the SOC 2 audit.

actionItems.task: Talk to Joe Furnari to move forward with the SOC 2 audit process.

expectedOutcome.description: The SOC 2 audit process is moving forward smoothly.

actionItems.task: Check calendar again for the invite to the meeting with Call Review's CEO and CIO.

expectedOutcome.description: Confirmation that the meeting invite has been received.

actionItems.task: Talk to Vinnie Micciche to (1) understand the distinction and complexity of the SOC 2 audit regarding VCONIC and the parent company structure, and (2) share the status of the SOC 2 audit.

expectedOutcome.description: Vinnie Micciche has a clearer understanding of the SOC 2 audit process and its implications.

actionItems.task: Validate the 100% of emails for the ICP and specific groups.

expectedOutcome.description: Confirmation that the emails for the ICP and specific groups are valid.

actionItems.task: Work on fixes to adjust Sophia to handle service calls more smoothly, addressing the feedback on awkward silence during weekend calls.

expectedOutcome.description: Sophia AI is handling service calls more effectively with reduced awkward silences.

actionItems.task: Be kept in the loop regarding the progress as the due diligence process runs through Call Review.

expectedOutcome.description: Regular updates on the progress of the Call Review due diligence process.

actionItems.task: Follow up on the invite list for the 11:00 a.m. call and ping the contact if Joe Furnari does not receive the email invite within a few more hours.

expectedOutcome.description: All invited attendees have received the calendar invite for the 11:00 a.m. call.

actionItems.task: Kevin Breen will verify the metabase Volley numbers against Volley.

tensions: Thomas Howe dismissed questions about VCON schema versions as a "red herring".

Sergey Lyapustin reported that internal testing occurred under ideal conditions with no background noise, which contrasts with actual customer calls that included background noise like a TV, causing the system to become "really confused".
Matt Watson cautioned that switching models would require retesting everything.
Vinnie Micciche's feedback to Michael Donovan regarding his sales deck was critical.
referencesToPast: Discussion refers to a document created and reviewed by Matt Watson and Sergey Lyapustin regarding VCON schema.
Reference to a Boston VCON meetup.
Reference to the Straw Life Cycle Center concept developed two or three years ago.
Mention of previous discussions with Jason on the pipeline call.
Reference to a previous discussion about an August target for SOC 2 compliance.
Mention of a previous demo for call review
